name: Angola
experiences: Fortaleza de São Miguel and Luanda bay viewpoints
Kissama National Park safari and Kwanza River wildlife
Serra da Leba switchbacks and Tundavala Fissure panoramas
Benguela coast seafood and Catumbela beach time
avoid: November to April is hotter, wetter, and muddier in Kissama, while escarpment viewpoints around Lubango can sit in cloud after storms.
Cards and ATMs get less reliable once you leave Luanda, so carry cash for park gates, fuel, and small-town restaurants.
TAAG domestic routes and long-distance buses are useful but not always daily, so lock the Luanda-Catumbela or Luanda-Lubango leg before finalizing hotels.
stopoverNote: If you only have a week, keep Luanda + Kissama + one of Benguela or Lubango, then use a TAAG domestic flight on one long leg instead of forcing every transfer by road.

cities.name: Luanda
cities.notes: Angola's capital is the easiest soft landing for SIM, cash, Fortaleza de São Miguel, the Marginal, and seafood dinners before you head south or into Kissama.

cities.name: Lubango
cities.notes: Lubango is the Huila highlands base for Tundavala Fissure, Cristo Rei, and the dramatic Serra da Leba road down toward the coast.

cities.name: Kissama National Park
cities.notes: Also spelled Quiçama, this is Angola's easiest flagship wildlife stop for giraffes, antelope, elephant habitat, and Kwanza River boat sections from a Luanda gateway.

cities.name: Benguela
cities.notes: Benguela mixes a slower colonial core, Praia Morena and Baía Azul beach time, seafood grills, and the Catumbela airport gateway for Angola's southwest coast.

itinerary.sim: Unitel and Africell are the easiest airport-counter options; budget about ₹900–₹2,000 for enough data for maps, WhatsApp, and bookings, with the strongest signal in Luanda and Benguela and weaker coverage once you are deeper inside Kissama.

Luanda.name: Luanda
Luanda.note: Use Luanda for the history hit, your easiest hotel stock, and the transport reset before Kissama or the southwest domestic flight.

days.theme: Historic hilltop Luanda, the fort museum, and a first Marginal evening

activities.name: Fortaleza de São Miguel / Museu Central das Forças Armadas admission
activities.tip: Traveler reports usually place foreigner entry around AOA 2,000-3,500, so carry small kwanza notes for the ticket desk.

activities.name: Marginal de Luanda waterfront walk
Museu da Moeda admission
Airport or central-city taxi rides

days.theme: Museums, island or bay downtime, and setting up the southbound leg

activities.name: Ilha do Cabo beach clubs or bay lunch stop
National Museum of Anthropology admission
TAAG Angola Airlines flight Luanda-Catumbela or Luanda-Lubango
activities.tip: Domestic fares climb fast on limited-frequency dates, so book the flight before locking in Benguela or Lubango hotels.

activities.name: Rental-car pickup for Kissama or the southwest road leg

Kissama National Park.name: Kissama National Park
Kissama National Park.note: Kissama works best as a one- or two-night safari break from Luanda, especially if you want a slower game drive plus the Kwanza River add-on.

days.theme: Drive down from Luanda, settle in, and take an afternoon wildlife loop

activities.name: Luanda-Kissama transfer by rental car or private driver
Kissama safari day package with guide, vehicle, and lunch
activities.tip: Recent Luanda day-tour pricing usually lands in the USD 120-220 band, so packaged safaris are the cleanest way to cover transport, guide, and reserve access.

activities.name: Park entry if billed separately on a self-drive visit
Sunset at the lodge or nearby Kwanza viewpoints

days.theme: Second game drive, river section, and a flexible return toward Luanda or the coast

activities.name: Morning game drive inside Kissama
Kwanza River boat safari add-on
Birding and lookout stops around the reserve roads
Shared road transfer back to Luanda or onward toward Cabo Ledo

Benguela.name: Benguela
Benguela.note: Benguela is the easiest coast-and-seafood reset after Luanda, and Catumbela airport makes it the simplest domestic hop before Lubango.

days.theme: Catumbela arrival, Praia Morena, and a relaxed first seafood dinner

activities.name: TAAG Angola Airlines flight Luanda-Catumbela
Airport taxi into Benguela or Lobito
Praia Morena beach walk and old-town promenade
Tuk-tuk or taxi hops between hotel, beachfront, and restaurants

days.theme: Beach time, colonial center, and planning the climb inland to Lubango

activities.name: Baía Azul beach stop by taxi or rental car
Benguela railway station and center walk
Intercity bus Benguela-Lubango
activities.tip: The budget overland option is slower but scenic, while a rental car gives you more freedom for Serra da Leba photo stops.

activities.name: Rental car for Benguela-Lubango road day

Lubango.name: Lubango
Lubango.note: Lubango deserves at least two nights so you can split the plateau views between Tundavala, Cristo Rei, and the Serra da Leba road without rushing the weather.

days.theme: Cristo Rei views, city reset, and an easy first evening in the highlands

activities.name: Cristo Rei Lubango viewpoint access
activities.tip: There is generally no formal ticket; if a local watches your car, a small cash tip is enough.

activities.name: Lubango city taxi rides
Museu Regional da Huíla admission
Sunset drinks back at the resort

days.theme: Tundavala Fissure, Serra da Leba switchbacks, and the signature Huila road day

activities.name: Tundavala Fissure viewpoint circuit
Serra da Leba pass viewpoints
activities.tip: The road and pull-offs are public, so budget only for fuel, a driver, or the occasional small parking tip.

activities.name: Private driver or rental-car day to Serra da Leba
Highlands picnic or coffee stop
